Retrospective: run — clean; no citable process defects on this branch. One adjacent pre-existing observation recorded for the next touch of that file: `docs/decisions/0012-gates-run-in-required-ci.md` ends with an empty `## Act` heading, and adr-lint has no empty-section check.

ADR restatement catch and the 0064/0065 id-gap handling verified as process working correctly, not defects.

*Disclosure: written by Claude (Claude Code) under the direction of the repo own

Retrospective: run — finding 1 (vacuous CI pass + the python defect it hid) fixed in this PR, its structural half filed as #198; finding 2 (ADR 0066 drafted over the lite budget, caught by the post-edit hook) recorded as a first instance, no new rule — the measure-first rule already has two homes and a working rung-2 backstop.

It verified the parallel-id pattern (0067 on the concurrent PR, 0068 here) is the adr-template.md mechanism working as designed, and noted the codepoints-vs-bytes measuring trap is already remedied by the ADR 0067 PR's doc-budgets.md fix.

*Disclosure: written by Claude (Claude Code) under the direction of the repo owner.*

Retrospective: skipped-owner-dictated-record-edit — the owner decided the content live in conversation; a process retrospective of transcription adds ceremony, not information. (Judgment call made under the new CLAUDE.md section this PR adds.)

*Disclosure: written by Claude (Claude Code) under the direction of the repo owner.*

Retrospective: skipped-owner-dictated-record-edit — mechanical implementation of the owner's live decision, same basis as PR #208.

*Disclosure: written by Claude (Claude Code) under the direction of the repo owner.*

Run (2 findings). Finding 1 (same-PR README/results contradiction) landed here as ADR 0070 + the benchmarks/README.md rule line. Finding 2 (CLAUDE.md mandate to quote verifying commands in issue bodies) NOT adopted: 2 confirmed instances, neither reached main, and CLAUDE.md's ~100 remaining budget chars are earmarked for the #212 stale-plugin-cache fold-in — advisory-strength finding, muda-assesse

Run (4 findings): 3 + 4 folded in above; 1 split to its own PR; 2 skipped with rationale. It also corrected a session-level misreading: current main has NO every-PR retrospect mandate — the reminder this session obeyed came from the stale 0.5.4 plugin cache enforcing retired policy, which finding 3's CLAUDE.md reword now names.

Closes #212

*Disclosure: written by Claude (Claude Code) under the d

Run (3 findings, all verified against the artifacts): (1) the canonical grid template has a concurrent-writer gap (read-then-run skip, no cross-process lock) — the $0.78 duplicate-pilot cost realized it; (2) judge.py lets subprocess.TimeoutExpired escape its own JudgeError contract and has no retry, unlike generation — the mid-pass grading crash realized it; (3) ceiling_usd vs the >2x stop rule ha
